title: Greetings & Introductions
description: Learn how to greet people and introduce yourself in everyday situations.
content: Hello / Hi — informal greeting used with friends and peers.
Good morning / Good afternoon / Good evening — formal time-based greetings.
How are you? — Common question. Reply: "I'm fine, thank you. And you?"
Nice to meet you — Used when meeting someone for the first time.
My name is... / I'm... — Two ways to introduce yourself.
Where are you from? — Ask about someone's origin. Reply: "I'm from Vietnam."

title: At the Restaurant
description: Essential phrases for ordering food and drinks at a restaurant.
content: A table for two, please. — Request a table when arriving.
Can I see the menu? — Ask to view available dishes.
I'd like to order... — Polite way to place an order.
What do you recommend? — Ask the waiter for suggestions.
Could I have the bill, please? — Request the check when finished.
Is service included? — Ask about tipping policy.

title: Present Simple Tense
description: Master the most fundamental English tense for daily communication.
content: Use Present Simple for habits, routines, and general truths.
Structure: Subject + base verb (add -s/-es for he/she/it).
I work / You work / He works / She works / It works / We work / They work.
Negative: Subject + do/does + not + base verb. "I don't like coffee."
Questions: Do/Does + subject + base verb? "Do you speak English?"
Time expressions: always, usually, often, sometimes, never, every day.

title: Travel & Directions
description: Navigate cities, ask for directions, and use public transport.
content: Excuse me, how do I get to...? — Ask for directions politely.
Go straight / Turn left / Turn right — Basic direction words.
It's about 10 minutes on foot. — Describe distance and time.
Where is the nearest bus stop? — Find public transport.
One ticket to..., please. — Buy transport tickets.
Is this seat taken? — Ask before sitting down.

title: Past Simple Tense
description: Talk about completed actions and events in the past.
content: Use Past Simple for actions completed at a specific time in the past.
Regular verbs: add -ed (walk → walked, play → played).
Irregular verbs: memorize forms (go → went, eat → ate, see → saw).
Negative: Subject + did + not + base verb. "She didn't come."
Questions: Did + subject + base verb? "Did you enjoy the movie?"
Time expressions: yesterday, last week, ago, in 2020.

title: Business Email Writing
description: Write professional emails for workplace communication.
content: Subject line: Be clear and specific. "Meeting Request — Project Update"
Opening: "Dear Mr./Ms. [Name]," or "Hi [First Name]," for informal.
Purpose: State your reason early. "I am writing to inquire about..."
Body: Keep paragraphs short. One idea per paragraph.
Closing: "Best regards," / "Sincerely," / "Thank you,"
Proofread before sending. Check spelling, tone, and attachments.

title: Airport & Flight Announcements
description: Understand common airport and flight announcements in English.
content: "Now boarding flight..." — Passengers with this flight should go to the gate.
"Final call for passengers..." — Last chance to board the plane.
"Please proceed to gate number..." — Go to the specified gate.
"We are now preparing for departure." — The plane is about to leave.
"Please fasten your seatbelt." — Safety instruction before takeoff.
"Ladies and gentlemen, welcome aboard." — Standard greeting from cabin crew.

title: Daily Life Vocabulary
description: Essential words for describing your daily routine and activities.
content: Wake up — Get out of bed in the morning.
Get dressed — Put on clothes.
Commute — Travel to work or school.
Have lunch — Eat the midday meal.
Hang out — Spend time relaxing with friends.
Go to bed — Prepare to sleep at night.

title: Hotel Check-in
description: Phrases for checking into a hotel and requesting services.
content: "I have a reservation under the name..." — Tell the receptionist your booking.
"Could I have a room with a view?" — Request a specific room type.
"What time is checkout?" — Ask about leaving time.
"Is breakfast included?" — Check if meals are part of your stay.
"Could you call a taxi for me?" — Request transport help.
"The Wi-Fi password, please." — Ask for internet access.

title: Job Interview Skills
description: Key phrases and strategies for English job interviews.
content: "Tell me about yourself." — Give a brief professional summary (1-2 minutes).
"What are your strengths?" — Highlight relevant skills with examples.
"Why do you want this job?" — Connect your goals with the company.
"Do you have any questions?" — Always prepare 2-3 thoughtful questions.
"I'm very interested in this opportunity." — Show enthusiasm.
Follow up with a thank-you email within 24 hours.
